mission.title: Install and Configure Ollama
mission.description: Ollama is an AI platform for serving large language models (LLMs) such as Kimi-K2.5 and Gemma. It allows users to easily deploy and interact with various models through a simple command-line interface. Key trade-offs include the need for sufficient computational resources and the requirement to manage model versions effectively.

steps.title: Install the Ollama CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Ollama command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the models.

```bash

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

irm https://ollama.com/install.ps1 | iex

```

> Use this command for Linux and macOS.
> Use this command for Windows.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your preferred region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create ollama-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region as needed.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials ollama-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Make sure to match the region used in the previous step.

steps.title: Verify the cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy Ollama on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Ollama application on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add ollama https://ollama.com/helm

helm install ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama --create-namespace

```

> Add the Ollama Helm repository.
> Install Ollama in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Post-installation verification
steps.description:
Verify that the Ollama deployment is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n ollama

```

> Ensure all pods are in 'Running' status.

steps.title: Cost warning
steps.description: Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources allocated.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Ollama
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Ollama deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall ollama --namespace ollama

gcloud container clusters delete ollama-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This will remove the Ollama deployment.
> This will delete the entire cluster and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ollama
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade your Ollama installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n ollama

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you want to drain.
> This command upgrades Ollama to the specified version.
> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you drained earlier.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that your kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials ollama-cluster --region=us-central1' to reset your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Ollama image could not be pulled from the repository.

**Fix:**
Check your internet connection and ensure the image exists in the repository.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means there is an issue with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n ollama' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the Ollama deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved API endpoints.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes in 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
